mnemonics: PEARSON: Pushdown Automata, Equivalence, Ambiguity, Regular vs Context-Free, Syntax Trees, Operations, Normal Forms
keyPoints: Pushdown Automata (PDA) extend finite automata with a stack for context-free languages.
A context-free grammar (CFG) is ambiguous if a string has more than one parse tree.
The equivalence between CFGs and PDAs is established through conversion algorithms.
The Pumping Lemma for context-free languages is used to prove certain languages are not context-free.
Normal forms like Chomsky Normal Form (CNF) and Greibach Normal Form (GNF) simplify grammar analysis.
commonMistakes: Confusing deterministic PDAs (DPDAs) with non-deterministic PDAs; not all context-free languages are deterministic.
Assuming the Pumping Lemma for regular languages applies to context-free languages; they have separate lemmas.
Forgetting that operations closed under regular languages (e.g., intersection) are not necessarily closed under context-free languages.
